description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by four rusted metal hoops. The wood appears weathered and dark, with visible grain and subtle discoloration, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The metal bands are oxidized, showing patches of black and reddish-brown rust, which adds to the rustic, utilitarian character of the object. The top and bottom are flat and made of the same wood as the sides, with no visible openings or spouts. There are no handles or decorative features, emphasizing its functional design. This type of barrel is commonly used for storage or transport of liquids or dry goods in urban, industrial, or outdoor settings.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with rusted metal hoops and a bulging cylindrical shape, showing signs of age and outdoor use.
description_view_0: The front view shows the full bulging profile of the barrel, highlighting the wood grain and the rusted metal hoops, with the top surface fully visible.
description_view_1: This view displays the barrel from a slightly rotated angle, revealing more of the right side and the curvature of the staves, with consistent weathering on the metal bands.
description_view_2: The side view emphasizes the symmetry of the barrel, with the metal hoops and wood grain evenly distributed and the top surface still visible.
description_view_3: This view shows the opposite side, with similar features as the other views, but the lighting accentuates the texture of the wood and the rust on the nearest metal band.
